preconditions: A 96-register current frame makes logical r32-r127 accessible
PSR.bn remains in the ordinary bank-zero view
inputs: A distinct signed-imm8 value equal to each logical register number r1-r127
expected_results: A second guest pass reads every register and compares it with its unique literal
The final debugger image independently repeats all 127 comparisons
allowed_results: Every r1-r127 value equals its register number and all associated NaT bits are clear
cases.committed_prefix: Each stopped write and later stopped comparison commits independently
cases.restart_action: Not exercised for legal current-frame operands

oracle.derivation: The register number itself is the literal value; a separately encoded compare pass and host image parser must both reproduce the identity mapping

limitations: Rotating GR renaming and interruption bank switching are separate REG-005/REG-009 closure units and are not claimed here

preconditions: PSR.dfl and PSR.dfh are clear
FR0/FR1 constants are covered by REG-004

cases.restart_action: Not exercised for enabled legal FR operands

result_observations: 126 exact GETF.SIG results across six processes

oracle.derivation: Each FR is assigned its literal index through SETF.SIG; complete-file initialization before every chunk detects aliased destinations or sources

limitations: This row covers the rrb.fr-zero index space; rotating renaming is a separate REG-010 closure unit

preconditions: CFM.rrb.pr is zero
PR0 constant behavior is covered by REG-004

required_unchanged_state: GRs outside declared setup and results
cases.committed_prefix: Each packed predicate image commits as one stopped transfer

oracle.derivation: Bit j of predicate n is defined as bit j of n, giving a mathematically unique six-bit signature for all 63 writable predicates

limitations: This row covers the rrb.pr-zero broadside view; rotating renaming is a separate REG-010 closure unit

preconditions: All MOV GR-to-BR and BR-to-GR encodings are legal

expected_results: Each BR-to-GR round trip and the final branch-register image preserves the matching value

cases.restart_action: Not exercised for NaT-clear legal sources

limitations: Indirect target alignment and branch execution are covered by BR rows, not this register-index row

preconditions: Execution begins in bank 0
PSR.ic is set before provoking the precise interruption
The interruption vector saves IPSR.bn and enters with PSR.bn clear
inputs: Distinct values in every banked GR16-GR31 of both banks
A NaT in bank-0 r16 and a NaT in bank-1 r31
A predicated illegal equal-target compare in slot 1
expected_results: The interruption handler observes bank 0 while IPSR.bn records bank 1
RFI restores bank 1 and suppressing the faulting predicate resumes at the interrupted bundle
BSW.0 returns to the original bank-0 values
Both banks preserve their independent NaT state
allowed_results: Exact one-to-one value and NaT preservation for all GR16-GR31 in both banks
permitted_state_changes: Both banked GR16-GR31 files and their NaT bits
PSR.bn, IPSR.bn, IIP, predicates, and declared observation GRs
expected_faults: Illegal Operation fault from the predicated equal-target compare
fault_priority: The illegal-operation fault is the sole enabled interruption source
cases.committed_prefix: Slot 0 commits before the slot-1 precise fault; slot 2 does not commit until RFI resumes with its predicate clear
cases.restart_action: The handler clears the qualifying predicate and RFI resumes the interrupted bundle in bank 1
applicable_variants: all sixteen values and NaT endpoints per bank

result_observations: full final bank-0 HMP GR and NaT image

oracle.derivation: The two banks receive disjoint arithmetic sequences and opposite NaT endpoints; handler and resumed code independently validate the architecturally selected view

limitations: Nested interruptions, taken-branch interruption collection, and MP bank visibility are separate closure units

citation.anchor: Registers are rotated towards larger register numbers

preconditions: PSR.dfl and PSR.dfh are clear
CFM.rrb.fr begins at zero
inputs: Unique integer significands 1 through 96 in f32-f127
Ninety-six qualifying WTOP rotations
expected_results: After one rotation every logical f32-f127 maps to the immediately preceding physical value with wraparound
At every remaining RRB base the low and high logical endpoints match the modulo-96 oracle
After 96 rotations the complete logical file returns to its initial mapping and RRB.FR is zero
permitted_state_changes: declared GR and predicate observation state
cases.committed_prefix: Each stopped WTOP and validation transfer commits independently

result_observations: guest exact comparisons for all indices after steps 1 and 96
endpoint comparisons after every intermediate step

oracle.derivation: Literal significand n labels physical f(31+n); expected logical values are computed solely by modulo-96 arithmetic

limitations: Floating-point arithmetic and disabled-FR faults are covered by separate rows

citation.anchor: Registers are rotated towards larger register numbers

preconditions: A 96-register frame is allocated
CFM.rrb.gr begins at zero
inputs: Every legal SOR size from 8 through 96 registers
Unique values in every member of the selected rotating region
A NaT on the lowest physical rotating register
One qualifying WTOP rotation per size
expected_results: For every legal SOR, all logical rotating GRs advance by one with the highest wrapping to the lowest
Every stacked GR above the selected SOR retains its unique literal
The NaT advances with its value from logical r32 to r33
CFM encodes the selected SOR and exact modulo-SOR RRB.GR result
allowed_results: The exact modulo-SOR value and NaT permutation for each of twelve SOR sizes
permitted_state_changes: GR32 through GR(31+SOR) and their NaT bits
required_unchanged_state: Memory except declared NaT source data
Stacked GRs above the selected SOR
cases.committed_prefix: Each stopped initialization, WTOP, and comparison commits independently

oracle.derivation: Each physical register is labeled by a distinct literal; the expected view after one rotation is calculated by modulo-SOR indexing rather than emulator state

limitations: RSE frame growth, spill/fill, and stacked-register allocation faults remain separate closure units

citation.anchor: Registers are rotated towards larger register numbers

preconditions: CFM.rrb.pr begins at zero
Predicate p0 remains the architectural true constant
inputs: Six independent bit-signature codewords across p16-p63
Forty-eight qualifying WTOP rotations per codeword
expected_results: After one WTOP, p16 contains the injected false value and p17-p63 contain the prior p16-p62 values
At every remaining RRB base p16 and p63 match the independent shift-and-zero-fill oracle
After 48 rotations all rotating predicates are false and RRB.PR returns to zero
allowed_results: The exact 48-step shift and WTOP false-injection sequence for every signature codeword
cases.committed_prefix: Each stopped predicate transfer, WTOP, and comparison commits independently

result_observations: endpoint comparisons after every intermediate step
final zeroed packed PR image and CFM

limitations: Loop-branch predicate injection and epilog-count behavior are covered by branch-loop rows

preconditions: Execution is at CPL0
PSR.ic is enabled for precise collected-fault restart
RSC.mode is zero
A guest CPUID[4] read confirms ru bit 3 is clear, making optional AR45 reserved
inputs: Every selector ar0-ar127
M-unit and I-unit register-form reads and writes
Enabled p1 and false p1 qualifications
Zero enabled-write value, nonzero false-write value, and poison read destination
expected_results: All 128 selectors match the independent Table 3-3 M-only, I-only, reserved, or ignored partition
Every legal enabled read/write completes
Every reserved or wrong-unit enabled access takes precise Illegal Operation
Both ignored ranges are legal in either unit, discard writes, and read zero
allowed_results: Exactly the Table 3-3 selector/unit classification for all 1,024 read/write/qualification combinations
permitted_state_changes: BSPSTORE/RNAT/RSC state from legal zero writes
required_unchanged_state: False-qualified access destinations and architectural AR state
fault_priority: Selector/unit/read-only legality is evaluated only after the qualifying predicate is true
cases.committed_prefix: Each selector access is isolated by stops; a fault commits only its completed setup prefix and leaves the destination poisoned
cases.restart_action: The common handler validates IIP and ISR, clears p1, and RFI re-executes the same access nullified before continuing
applicable_variants: defined, reserved, ignored, read-only, and wrong-unit classes

oracle.derivation: Four literal selector sets are transcribed from Intel Volume 1 Table 3-3; expected access disposition is computed from set membership and execution unit without consulting QEMU tables

limitations: Nonzero field masks, CPL/PSR.si privilege, RSE-mode restrictions, and named AR value semantics remain separate REG-007/REG-008 closure units

citation.anchor: An access to an application register from the wrong unit type causes an Illegal Operation fault

preconditions: Execution is at CPL0 with PSR.ic enabled
Every enabled write uses a non-reserved zero value
RSC.mode remains zero
CPUID[4].ru is clear
inputs: Every M/I selector and read/write direction
An otherwise identical false-qualified access for every form
A nonzero false-write source and poison false-read destination
expected_results: Wrong-unit, reserved-selector, and enabled BSP-write forms fault without changing their destination
False qualification suppresses every selector/unit fault and preserves the read destination
Ignored registers accept both unit forms, discard writes, and return zero
Defined legal registers accept only their Table 3-3 execution unit
allowed_results: Exact Illegal Operation versus legal/no-effect classification with precise saved slot state for every selector form
permitted_state_changes: Zero writes to defined writable ARs
required_unchanged_state: Destination of every faulting or false-qualified read
Architectural state for reserved, ignored, read-only, and false-qualified writes
fault_priority: False qualification suppresses selector, execution-unit, and read-only checks
cases.committed_prefix: The stopped setup prefix commits; the faulting AR operation and its poisoned destination do not
cases.restart_action: The handler proves exact IIP/EI/code, clears the access predicate, and RFI completes the nullified retry exactly once

oracle.derivation: The Volume 1 selector partition is combined with Volume 3 mov-ar pseudocode: predicate first, unit/selector legality, read-only legality, ignored read zero, and ignored write discard

limitations: Reserved nonzero field patterns, NaT priority, privilege transitions, and individual named-AR masks are not claimed here

citation.anchor: The RUC register is reserved when this feature is not supported

preconditions: The selected CPU profile exposes CPUID register 4
Execution is at CPL0 with PSR.ic enabled
inputs: A guest indexed read of CPUID[4]
Enabled and false-qualified M/I reads and writes of AR45
expected_results: CPUID[4].ru bit 3 is zero
Every enabled AR45 access takes precise Illegal Operation
Every false-qualified AR45 access is inert
allowed_results: The optional RUC feature is consistently absent from both CPUID advertisement and AR45 selector behavior
fault_priority: The absent optional register is reserved after qualification
cases.committed_prefix: Only stopped setup state commits before each enabled AR45 fault
cases.restart_action: The handler validates exact IIP/ISR, clears p1, and RFI completes the nullified retry

oracle.derivation: Intel Volume 2 defines CPUID[4].ru as the RUC availability contract and requires AR45 to be reserved when it is clear

limitations: RUC counter monotonicity, write serialization, and PSR.si privilege apply only to profiles advertising the optional feature

preconditions: Execution is at CPL0
PSR.ic is clear for enabled accesses to CR16-CR27 and set for all other enabled accesses
The selected emulator profile does not implement optional CR4 ITO
inputs: Every selector cr0-cr127
M-unit MOV-to-CR and MOV-from-CR forms
Independent safe values for every writable defined register
A separate full-file initialization with distinct safe values before any readback
Poison destinations for faulting and nullified reads
expected_results: All 128 selectors match the Volume 2 defined, reserved, or optional-absent partition
CR26 IIB0 and CR27 IIB1 are defined interruption registers
Every defined writable selector round-trips its safe value after SRLZ.D
The full-file delayed readback detects aliased selector sources or destinations
Reserved selectors take precise Illegal Operation
allowed_results: Exactly the selected-profile Table 3-3 selector partition and declared normalized read values
permitted_state_changes: Timer, TLB, and interrupt-controller side effects architecturally associated with legal safe writes
fault_priority: PSR.ic access restriction applies only to enabled interruption-register accesses
cases.committed_prefix: Each CR access is isolated by stops; SRLZ.D follows every completed write
cases.restart_action: The common handler validates exact IIP/ISR, clears p1, and RFI re-executes the access nullified

oracle.derivation: The selector sets and CR26/CR27 endpoints are transcribed from Intel Volume 2 Table 3-3; safe values are constrained by the published register formats rather than QEMU state

limitations: Optional ITO feature advertisement through PAL is a separate REG-003/PAL closure unit
Named field-mask and reset-value semantics remain separate REG-001/REG-007/REG-008 closure units

citation.anchor: Writes to read-only registers (IVR, IRR0-3) result in an Illegal Operation fault

preconditions: Execution is at CPL0
PSR.ic starts enabled and is serialized after every transition
No external interrupt is pending in each fresh shard
inputs: Enabled and false-qualified reads and writes for all 128 selectors
Additional enabled PSR.ic=1 reads and writes for every CR16-CR27 defined selector
Nonzero false-write values and poisoned read destinations
expected_results: All 512 base selector/direction/qualification forms have the exact legal or Illegal Operation disposition
All 22 enabled interruption-register forms fault with PSR.ic=1 and their matching PSR.ic=0 forms complete
IVR and IRR0-IRR3 writes fault while reads return the spurious vector or empty request image
False qualification suppresses reserved, read-only, and PSR.ic checks
required_unchanged_state: Reserved and read-only CR state on faulting writes
Destination of every faulting or false-qualified read
Architectural CR state on every false-qualified write
fault_priority: Predicate qualification precedes selector, read-only, and PSR.ic legality
The PSR.ic restriction precedes completed access to CR16-CR27
cases.committed_prefix: Only the stopped setup prefix commits before each enabled illegal access
cases.restart_action: The handler proves exact IIP/EI/code, clears p1, and RFI completes a nullified retry once

oracle.derivation: Volume 2 defines the read-only and interruption-state access restrictions; the generator combines those literal sets with predicate and direction without consulting emulator decode or helpers

limitations: The operation-wide CPL0 privilege rule is already exercised by the system-instruction batch and is not redundantly expanded to every selector here
Individual named-register reserved-field patterns remain separate REG-007/REG-008 closure units
